title: NOC 62020 – Food Service Supervisors | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 62020 is one of the most popular and accessible pathways for professionals entering the Canadian hospitality sector. Food service supervisors are essential in hospitals, cafeterias, and restaurants, ensuring that quality and safety standards are met every day. As a TEER 2 occupation, this role offers a direct and reliable path to Permanent Residency, especially through the BC PNP Skills Immigration and Alberta Opportunity Stream.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level2Major Group62 – Retail sales and service supervisorsSub-Major Group620 – Retail sales and service supervisorsMinor Group6202 – Service supervisorsNOC Code62020

duties: Supervise, direct, and coordinate the activities of workers who prepare and serve food.
Establish methods to meet work schedules and maintain high service standards.
Maintain records of stock, repairs, sales, and waste.
Train staff in job duties, sanitation, and safety procedures.
Ensure that food and service meet strict quality control standards.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Completion of a community college program in hospitality or food service management OR several years of experience in food preparation is required.
job_outlook: The outlook is Moderate to Favourable. While the median wage is lower than managerial roles, the sheer volume of vacancies (projected 53,000+) makes this an extremely secure career choice for new immigrants.
labour_market_demand: Canada is currently facing a critical shortage of food service supervisors. This has led to highly competitive salaries and increased support for LMIA-based hiring. Healthcare facilities and catering companies, in particular, are looking for international talent to lead their kitchen and service teams.
Rapid PR Pathway: One of the most common occupations for successful PR transitions.
Diverse Workplaces: Opportunities in healthcare, education, and corporate catering.
Stable Industry: Food services are an essential part of the Canadian economy.
pathways: BC PNP Skills Immigration: Dedicated stream for hospitality workers.
Alberta Opportunity Stream: Fast PR for those working in Alberta.
CEC (Express Entry): Perfect for those with 12 months of Canadian experience.
